title: zosterophyllanthos troglodytes
intro: Zosterophyllanthos troglodytes, also known as the 'rock fig', is a species of flowering plant in the family Cunoniaceae. It is a shrub native to Western Australia, known for its medicinal properties which include antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems, offering relief from pain and inflammation. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the Zosterophyllanthos troglodytes plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness its medicinal benefits.
